more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe
more.description: Zimbabwe stands at an unusual intersection of necessity and opportunity. On one hand, the pressures are unmistakable: energy insecurity, climate variability, industrial stagnation, and constrained capital. On the other hand, the country possesses the raw ingredients of a green transition: abundant sunlight, agricultural depth, mineral wealth, and a youthful population increasingly attuned to technology and […]The post Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zimbabwe emerges as SADC’s second largest economy
more.description: Zimbabwe’s economy has been officially rebased to an estimated US$52,4 billion for 2025, positioning the country as the second-largest economy in Southern Africa after South Africa. The revised figures, released by Zimbabwe National Statistics Agency, follow a comprehensive economic census conducted in 2023 that captured previously underreported sectors, particularly the informal economy. Treasury, under Permanent [...] The post Zimbabwe emerges as SADC’s second largest economy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Firearm Found at Plumtree Border Post: How Illegal Guns are Flooding Zimbabwe as ED2030 Agenda Turns Dangerous
more.description: PLUMTREE – On a sweltering afternoon at the Plumtree Border Post, a routine customs inspection by Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (ZIMRA) officials took a turn from the mundane to the chilling. Amidst the usual clutter of cross-border trade—second-hand clothes, basic groceries, and electronic parts—officers stumbled upon a concealed, undeclared firearm hidden deep within a traveller’s suitcase. more.title: Beyond GDP: Rethinking Economic Development in Zimbabwe for a New Era of Shared Prosperity
more.description: Zimbabwe’s economic debate has long been framed around a single, dominant question: how to grow the economy. For decades, policymakers have measured progress through traditional indicators, GDP growth rates, export volumes, and fiscal balances, often assuming that improvements in these metrics would naturally translate into better livelihoods for ordinary citizens. more.title: Reimagining Zimbabwe’s Industrial Future: Digitisation, Sustainability and the Rise of the Smart Factory
more.description: Zimbabwe’s manufacturing sector stands at a defining crossroads. Once the industrial heartbeat of Southern Africa, the country’s production base has, over the past two decades, endured structural decline driven by currency instability, infrastructure deficits, and shifting global value chains.
